headline: Waist-high SE windswell, clean early then cross-on by lunch — log it at dawn.
today: Short-period SE at good angle giving you 2ft on the sets, and the N/ENE early wind keeps it clean through the morning. Get on it dawn to mid-morning around that outgoing tide before the ESE fills in and puts it cross-on for the afternoon and evening. Log or groveler, nothing you need to paddle hard for.

daysToWatch.note: Light ENE morning wind means glassy conditions on a prime angle — small at 1.5-2ft but the cleanest window before SSE onshore ruins the afternoon. Log session.
Onshore SSE all day and building junk — skip unless you just want to get wet.
The one to rearrange for: 2.6-3.9ft at 5.5-6s and light winds dawn to afternoon. Fair angle but the size and glass make it the best day of the stretch. Bring the shortboard.
N offshore all day holding 2ft-plus clean. Fun follow-up session, groveler or log.
